methods.method_name: In Vitro Pollen Tube Growth and Treatment
methods.description: Procedure for culturing pollen in vitro, treating with various compounds (flavonoids, NPA, NOA, EGTA), and analyzing pollen tube growth and calcium fluorescence using microscopy and image analysis.

steps.description: Harvested pollen grains were cultured on solid germination medium (GM, 10% sucrose, 0.01% boric acid, 1% agar, pH 6.5) as described by Meng et al. with minor modification 34 .
steps.searchable_summary: Collect pollen grains from plants for in vitro culture.

steps.description: Harvested pollen grains were cultured on solid germination medium (GM, 10% sucrose, 0.01% boric acid, 1% agar, pH 6.5) as described by Meng et al. with minor modification 34 .
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare solid germination medium (GM) with specified composition.

steps.description: For compound treatment, 1 μM kaempferol, kaempferol derivatives, quercetin derivatives, or cyanidin 3-O-galactoside dissolved in Dimethylsulfoxide (DMSO) were added into the GM and mixed uniformly before the media solidified.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare compound-treated germination medium by adding flavonoid compounds dissolved in DMSO to liquid GM before solidification.
inputs: Flavonoid compounds (kaempferol, kaempferol derivatives, quercetin derivatives, cyanidin 3-O-galactoside)

steps.description: The DMSO solution without additives was used as the control.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare control germination medium by adding only DMSO to liquid GM.

steps.description: Harvested pollen grains were cultured on solid germination medium...
steps.searchable_summary: Plate harvested pollen grains onto solidified treated and control germination media.

steps.description: The pollen was incubated at 28°C in a dark and humid environment for 4 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate pollen cultures under specified conditions (28°C, dark, humid) for 4 hours.

steps.description: The pollen of M. domestica 'Fuji' was used for 1-Nnaphthylphthalamic acid (NPA, final concentration, 40 μM), β-naphthoxyacetic acid (NOA, final concentration 20 μM), and EGTA (final concentration 10 mM) treatments.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare solutions for NPA, NOA, and EGTA treatments at specified final concentrations.

steps.description: The pollen of M. domestica 'Fuji' was used for 1-Nnaphthylphthalamic acid (NPA, final concentration, 40 μM), β-naphthoxyacetic acid (NOA, final concentration 20 μM)... treatments.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply NPA and NOA treatments to pollen cultures.

steps.description: In our preliminary experiment, it was found that EGTA fully inhibited the germination of pollen, so the EGTA treatments were carried out after 2 h of culture of pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply EGTA treatment to pollen cultures after 2 hours of incubation.

steps.description: The calcium fluorescence and pollen tube length were observed with a fluorescence microscope (BX51 + DP70, Olympus).
steps.searchable_summary: Observe calcium fluorescence in pollen using a fluorescence microscope.

steps.description: The calcium fluorescence and pollen tube length were observed with a fluorescence microscope (BX51 + DP70, Olympus).
steps.searchable_summary: Observe pollen tube length using a fluorescence microscope.

steps.description: The length of pollen tube was measured by ImageJ.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the length of pollen tubes from microscopic images using ImageJ software.

steps.description: Measurement of the length of pollen tubes was carried out on five replicates.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform pollen tube length measurements on five biological replicates.
outputs: Pollen tube length data from 5 replicates

steps.description: For each replicate, 50 pollen grains were randomly selected.
steps.searchable_summary: Randomly select 50 pollen grains per replicate for measurement.

methods.method_name: In Vivo Pollen Tube Growth Analysis
methods.description: Procedure for analyzing pollen tube growth within the style after pollination, involving tissue collection, fixation, softening, staining, and visualization using fluorescence microscopy.

steps.description: Pollen grains from M. domestica 'Granny Smith' were collected...
steps.searchable_summary: Collect pollen grains from donor plants for pollination.

steps.description: ...to pollinate 'Royal Gala' and its MYB10 overexpressed transgenic flowers.
steps.searchable_summary: Pollinate recipient flowers ('Royal Gala', MYB10 transgenic) with collected pollen.

steps.description: The pistils were harvested 24, 48, and 72 h after pollination...
steps.searchable_summary: Harvest pistils from pollinated flowers at specified time points (24, 48, 72 h).

steps.description: ...and then fixed in FAA fixative solution (70% alcohol: acetic acid: formaldehyde = 18:1:1) for 12 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Fix harvested pistils in FAA fixative solution for 12 hours.
inputs: FAA fixative solution (70% alcohol: acetic acid: formaldehyde = 18:1:1)

steps.description: The fixed pistils were washed with distilled water three times...
steps.searchable_summary: Wash fixed pistils with distilled water three times.

steps.description: ...following an incubation in 2 M NaOH softening solution for 24 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate washed pistils in 2 M NaOH softening solution for 24 hours.

steps.description: After being washed with distilled water three times...
steps.searchable_summary: Wash softened pistils with distilled water three times.

steps.description: ...the pistils were stained in 0.1% aniline blue (0.1% K 3 PO 4 buffer) for 24 h in the dark.
steps.searchable_summary: Stain washed pistils in 0.1% aniline blue solution (in K3PO4 buffer) for 24 hours in the dark.

steps.description: Pollen tubes in the style were visualized with a BX51 + DP70 fluorescence microscope (Olympus Corporation, Japan) as described by Kho and Baer 53 with minor modifications.
steps.searchable_summary: Visualize stained pollen tubes within the style using a fluorescence microscope.
outputs: Microscopic images of pollen tubes in style
